rows.definition: The kernel itself: the in-process adjudication system that runs the decision chain and admits results, launched as `fak guard`.
rows.distinction: A SYSTEM - NOT a gate (one decision point inside it) and NOT the trunk/repo guards (git-side policy checks).

rows.definition: One decision point inside a guard, splitting by WHEN it fires: pre-call adjudicators, post-call result admitters, and git-hook gates.
rows.distinction: A POINT, not the system - NOT the guard (the whole kernel) and NOT any single gate kind (adjudicator vs admitter vs hook).

rows.definition: A post-call gate: inspects a tool RESULT after execution and admits / quarantines / transforms it (ctxmmu, normgate, secretgate).
rows.distinction: Fires POST-call on result bytes - NOT the adjudicator (pre-call, on the request) and NOT a git-hook gate (commit boundary).

rows.definition: A commit-boundary check at git pre-commit / commit-msg (gate_brokenlink, gate_secretshape, gate_provenance, ...).
rows.distinction: A STATIC commit-boundary check - NOT gitgate (a runtime git-command prefilter) and NOT a result admitter (post-tool).

rows.definition: A git hook that refuses commits made off the trunk (the OFF_TRUNK law), keeping all work on main.
rows.distinction: A branch-STATE policy (refuse OFF_TRUNK) - NOT repo guard (write-target policy) and NOT gitgate (git-command prefilter).

rows.definition: A PreToolUse hook that refuses Bash/Write/Edit calls targeting paths outside the workspace tree.
rows.distinction: A write-TARGET policy (block escaping the tree) - NOT trunk guard (branch state) and NOT gitgate (git subcommands).

rows.definition: An adjudicator rung that prefilters git hazards (force-push, --no-verify, rebase -i) from shell tool calls at runtime.
rows.distinction: A runtime git-COMMAND prefilter - NOT git-hook gates (static commit-boundary checks) and NOT trunk guard (branch state).

rows.definition: An adjudicator that enforces RSI keep-or-revert verdicts on a code change based on witness-verified metric gain, never the candidate's own claim.
rows.distinction: Gates a CODE improvement on proven gain - NOT an L3 cache promotion gate (durability class) and NOT a result-admitter gate.

rows.definition: A ResultAdmitter that canonicalizes obfuscated payloads (base64, homoglyph, zero-width) before re-scanning for secrets.
rows.distinction: Detection-CLOSURE (decode then detect) - NOT secretgate (discovery-classification) and NOT ctxmmu (generic quarantine).
gaps: the individual result-admitter rungs are not yet written into the glossary

rows.definition: A ResultAdmitter that classifies discovered credentials as RESULT_SECRET_DISCOVERED events, distinct from injection poisoning.
rows.distinction: Discovery-CLASSIFICATION (mark credentials) - NOT normgate (detection-closure) and NOT ctxmmu (generic quarantine).
gaps: the individual result-admitter rungs are not yet written into the glossary

rows.definition: A rank-20 ResultAdmitter that stamps every tool result's taint by SOURCE (trusted-local vs untrusted-egress) and clamps the result's ShareScope DOWNWARD.
rows.distinction: A CONTROL-PLANE adjudication gate (taint by origin) - NOT a model gating WEIGHT (mlp.gate_proj et al.) and NOT the upward ScopeCeilingGate ceiling.

rows.definition: The result-side ShareScope CEILING gate (rank 21, above StampGate): confines cross-agent taint visibility to the declared scope boundary - the upward dual of StampGate's downward clamp.
rows.distinction: A CONTROL-PLANE adjudication gate (scope ceiling) - NOT a model gating projection and NOT StampGate (the downward source-stamp clamp).

rows.definition: The egress adjudicator that DENIES a tool call whose arguments carry untrusted taint into a configured sink, governed by a GatedSinks policy.
rows.distinction: A CONTROL-PLANE pre-dispatch adjudicator (deny tainted args into a sink) - NOT a model gating weight and NOT StampGate (the post-call result stamp).

rows.definition: A trust-gate REFUSAL REASON code: a context page that is sealed/tombstoned cannot be demand-paged back in.
rows.distinction: A refusal REASON string emitted by the context trust gate - NOT a gate TYPE you configure and NOT a model gating tensor.

rows.definition: The Policy preset that gates ALL egress sinks strictly: the GatedSinks configuration the SinkGate adjudicator enforces.
rows.distinction: A POLICY preset (data the SinkGate reads) - NOT the SinkGate adjudicator itself and NOT a model gating weight.

rows.definition: The FFN/SwiGLU GATE projection weight in an MLP layer; after SiLU it is multiplied with up_proj, distinct from down_proj.
rows.distinction: A NEURAL-NET FFN gating WEIGHT (SwiGLU) - NOTHING to do with adjudication gates (StampGate/SinkGate); distinct from gate_up_proj (the fused gate+up).

rows.definition: Qwen3.5 linear-attention QUERY gating projection (self_attn.q_gate_proj) in Gated-DeltaNet layers; the in_proj_z source absorbs it.
rows.distinction: A NEURAL-NET attention gating WEIGHT (Gated-DeltaNet query gate) - NOT an adjudication gate and NOT the FFN mlp.gate_proj.

rows.definition: The FUSED gate+up projection (mlp.gate_up_proj.weight): gate_proj and up_proj concatenated into one tensor the loader splits.
rows.distinction: A NEURAL-NET fused FFN gate+up weight the loader splits into gate_proj and up_proj - NOT an adjudication gate and NOT the standalone mlp.gate_proj.

rows.definition: The GGUF tensor name for the FFN gate weight (ffn_gate.weight), canonicalized to mlp.gate_proj.weight on load.
rows.distinction: The GGUF spelling of the FFN gate weight (loads to mlp.gate_proj) - NOT an adjudication gate; the same model gate as mlp.gate_proj under llama.cpp naming.

rows.definition: A Qwen3.5 CONFIG flag (attn_output_gate) enabling a sigmoid GATE on full-attention output logits.
rows.distinction: A MODEL CONFIG flag toggling sigmoid gating of attention output - NOT an adjudication gate and NOT a gating WEIGHT tensor like q_gate_proj.

rows.definition: The Qwen3.5 gated-RMSNorm compute (Qwen3_5RMSNormGated): x = weight * rmsnorm(x) * silu(gate), applied in place.
rows.distinction: A NEURAL-NET gated-normalization COMPUTE (RMSNorm scaled by a SiLU gate) - NOT an adjudication gate and NOT a gating WEIGHT (it consumes one).
